Module com.example.battleship
Package com.example.battleship.view.assets

Class BoardLabels3D

java.lang.Object
com.example.battleship.view.assets.BoardLabels3D
public final class BoardLabels3D extends Object
Builds the column-letter (A..J) and row-number (1..10) labels that sit just outside a board's two edges, like a spreadsheet's headers.

Each label is baked once into a small transparent-background image (via Node.snapshot(javafx.scene.SnapshotParameters, javafx.scene.image.WritableImage)) and applied as a texture on a flat 3D quad lying on the water plane -- the same safe technique used for the sky gradient: real 3D geometry with a texture, never a live 2D Text node sitting directly in the 3D scene graph.

Call build(Layout, Board) once per board (it returns a fresh Group each time, since a node can only belong to one parent) and add the result as a child of that board's own group, so the labels line up with that board's local 0..360 coordinates. The BoardLabels3D.Board argument picks which quad texture mapping to use -- the player's board and the machine's board sit on opposite edges of the field, so each needs its own confirmed-correct orientation rather than sharing one.

    Class
    Description
    static enum 
    BoardLabels3D.Board
    Which board these labels are for -- picks which quad texture mapping to use.
    static final record 
    BoardLabels3D.Layout
    Which edges the letters/numbers sit on, and which direction each one reads in.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static javafx.scene.Group
    build(BoardLabels3D.Layout layout, BoardLabels3D.Board board)
    Builds one full set of column-letter and row-number labels for a board, positioned in that board's own local 0..360 coordinates (relative to whatever group the caller adds the result to).
    private static javafx.scene.shape.MeshView
    buildFlatQuad(double width, double depth, float[] texCoords)
    A single flat rectangle (2 triangles, no thickness) lying in the X/Z plane.
    private static javafx.scene.shape.MeshView
    buildFlatQuadForMachine(double width, double depth)
    Flat quad for the machine's board labels.
    private static javafx.scene.shape.MeshView
    buildFlatQuadForPlayer(double width, double depth)
    Flat quad for the player's board labels: this texCoords mapping (both U and V flipped relative to the "obvious" one) is the confirmed-correct orientation for that board's position/camera angle.
    private static javafx.scene.shape.MeshView
    createLabel(String text, BoardLabels3D.Board board)
    Builds one label's flat textured quad, sized to keep the baked text image's own aspect ratio.
    private static javafx.scene.image.Image
    renderTextTexture(String text)
    Renders a single character/word to a small transparent-background image.

  • Method Details

    • build

      public static javafx.scene.Group build(BoardLabels3D.Layout layout, BoardLabels3D.Board board)
      Builds one full set of column-letter and row-number labels for a board, positioned in that board's own local 0..360 coordinates (relative to whatever group the caller adds the result to).
      Parameters:
      layout - which edges/reading order to use (see BoardLabels3D.Layout)
      board - which board these labels are for (picks the texture mapping)
      Returns:
      a fresh group containing every letter and number label

    • createLabel

      private static javafx.scene.shape.MeshView createLabel(String text, BoardLabels3D.Board board)
      Builds one label's flat textured quad, sized to keep the baked text image's own aspect ratio.

    • renderTextTexture

      private static javafx.scene.image.Image renderTextTexture(String text)
      Renders a single character/word to a small transparent-background image.

    • buildFlatQuadForPlayer

      private static javafx.scene.shape.MeshView buildFlatQuadForPlayer(double width, double depth)
      Flat quad for the player's board labels: this texCoords mapping (both U and V flipped relative to the "obvious" one) is the confirmed-correct orientation for that board's position/camera angle.

    • buildFlatQuadForMachine

      private static javafx.scene.shape.MeshView buildFlatQuadForMachine(double width, double depth)
      Flat quad for the machine's board labels. The machine board sits on the opposite side of the field from the player's, so it needs its own texCoords mapping rather than reusing the player's -- starting from the un-flipped mapping here; adjust this array the same way the player one was tuned if it still reads wrong.

    • buildFlatQuad

      private static javafx.scene.shape.MeshView buildFlatQuad(double width, double depth, float[] texCoords)
      A single flat rectangle (2 triangles, no thickness) lying in the X/Z plane.
